Apple___Apple_scab.symptoms: Dark olive-green to brown lesions on leaves and fruit, often with rough scabby patches.
Apple___Apple_scab.treatment: Remove infected debris and apply a recommended apple fungicide according to local agricultural guidance.
Apple___Apple_scab.prevention: Prune for airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, and remove fallen leaves after harvest.

Apple___Black_rot.symptoms: Circular brown leaf spots, fruit rot, and cankers on branches.
Apple___Black_rot.treatment: Prune infected wood, remove mummified fruit, and use fungicide where disease pressure is high.
Apple___Black_rot.prevention: Maintain orchard sanitation and avoid leaving infected fruit or limbs in the field.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.symptoms: Bright yellow-orange leaf spots and premature leaf drop in severe infections.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.treatment: Apply rust-labeled fungicide early in the season when infection risk is present.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.prevention: Reduce nearby cedar or juniper hosts when practical and select resistant cultivars.

Apple___healthy.prevention: Continue routine monitoring, pruning, balanced nutrition, and irrigation management.

Blueberry___healthy.prevention: Maintain acidic soil, balanced irrigation, and routine scouting.

Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White powdery growth on leaves and young shoots, sometimes with leaf distortion.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.treatment: Remove infected shoots and apply sulfur or other locally recommended fungicides.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.prevention: Improve canopy airflow and avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization.

Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.prevention: Continue pruning, orchard sanitation, and regular disease scouting.

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.symptoms: Long rectangular gray to tan lesions running parallel to leaf veins.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.treatment: Use fungicide on susceptible hybrids when disease pressure and crop stage justify treatment.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.prevention: Rotate crops, manage residue, and plant resistant hybrids.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.symptoms: Small reddish-brown pustules scattered across leaf surfaces.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.treatment: Apply fungicide if infection is severe on susceptible hybrids before yield loss occurs.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.prevention: Use resistant hybrids and monitor early in cool, humid conditions.

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.symptoms: Long cigar-shaped gray-green lesions that turn tan as they mature.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.treatment: Apply fungicide when lesions appear before or around tasseling under high disease pressure.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.prevention: Use resistant hybrids, rotate crops, and manage infected residue.

Corn_(maize)___healthy.prevention: Maintain balanced fertilization, irrigation, and field scouting.

Grape___Black_rot.symptoms: Brown circular leaf spots and shriveled black fruit mummies.
Grape___Black_rot.treatment: Remove infected fruit and apply grape fungicides according to local timing guidance.
Grape___Black_rot.prevention: Prune for airflow, remove mummified berries, and keep vineyard rows clean.

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).symptoms: Interveinal leaf striping, dark fruit spotting, and vine decline.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).treatment: Prune infected wood, protect pruning wounds, and remove severely affected vines.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).prevention: Avoid trunk injuries, sanitize tools, and use healthy planting material.

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.symptoms: Dark brown angular lesions and drying leaf tissue.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.treatment: Remove infected leaves and apply a recommended fungicide if disease spreads.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.prevention: Improve airflow, reduce leaf wetness, and maintain vineyard sanitation.

Grape___healthy.prevention: Continue canopy management, irrigation control, and regular scouting.

Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).symptoms: Blotchy yellow leaves, misshapen bitter fruit, twig dieback, and poor tree vigor.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).treatment: There is no reliable cure; remove infected trees where recommended and control psyllid vectors.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).prevention: Use certified disease-free nursery stock and monitor psyllid populations.

Peach___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small dark water-soaked lesions on leaves and fruit, often causing shot-hole leaf damage.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Use copper or other locally approved bactericides and remove heavily infected material.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Choose resistant cultivars, reduce leaf wetness, and avoid overhead irrigation.

Peach___healthy.prevention: Maintain pruning, balanced nutrition, and orchard sanitation.

Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Water-soaked leaf spots that darken and may cause defoliation or fruit lesions.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Remove infected plants when severe and use copper-based sprays where locally recommended.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use certified seed, rotate crops, avoid wet foliage, and sanitize tools.

Pepper,_bell___healthy.prevention: Continue balanced irrigation, nutrition, and pest scouting.

Potato___Early_blight.symptoms: Dark concentric target-like spots on older leaves.
Potato___Early_blight.treatment: Remove infected foliage where practical and apply labeled fungicides when disease pressure is high.
Potato___Early_blight.prevention: Rotate crops, avoid plant stress, and maintain proper spacing.

Potato___Late_blight.symptoms: Dark water-soaked lesions, white growth under humid conditions, and rapid canopy collapse.
Potato___Late_blight.treatment: Act quickly with locally recommended fungicides and destroy severely infected plants.
Potato___Late_blight.prevention: Use certified seed, avoid overhead irrigation, and monitor during cool wet weather.

Potato___healthy.prevention: Maintain crop rotation, clean seed, and routine field scouting.

Raspberry___healthy.prevention: Maintain pruning, airflow, and regular pest and disease monitoring.

Soybean___healthy.prevention: Continue balanced fertility, crop rotation, and routine field scouting.

Squash___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White powdery fungal patches on leaves and stems.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.treatment: Remove heavily infected leaves and apply labeled fungicides if disease is spreading.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.prevention: Improve airflow, avoid overcrowding, and use resistant varieties when available.

Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.symptoms: Purple spots that expand into scorched or dry leaf edges.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.treatment: Remove infected leaves and apply fungicide if recommended for local disease pressure.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.prevention: Improve spacing, reduce leaf wetness, and remove old foliage after harvest.

Strawberry___healthy.prevention: Maintain clean beds, proper spacing, and regular scouting.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small water-soaked leaf spots that turn dark, with possible fruit spotting.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Remove infected debris and use copper-based products where locally recommended.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use disease-free seed or transplants, avoid overhead irrigation, and rotate away from solanaceous crops.

Tomato___Early_blight.symptoms: Dark concentric rings on older leaves, yellowing, and lower-leaf defoliation.
Tomato___Early_blight.treatment: Remove affected lower leaves and apply labeled fungicide when disease spreads.
Tomato___Early_blight.prevention: Mulch to reduce soil splash, stake plants, rotate crops, and improve airflow.

Tomato___Late_blight.symptoms: Large water-soaked lesions, white growth in humid conditions, and rapid plant decline.
Tomato___Late_blight.treatment: Remove infected plants promptly and use appropriate fungicides under extension guidance.
Tomato___Late_blight.prevention: Avoid prolonged leaf wetness, increase spacing, and scout during cool wet weather.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.symptoms: Yellow patches on upper leaf surfaces with olive-gray mold on undersides.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.treatment: Remove infected leaves and apply fungicide if humidity-driven spread continues.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.prevention: Ventilate greenhouses, reduce humidity, and avoid wet foliage.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.symptoms: Many small circular spots with gray centers and dark borders on lower leaves.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.treatment: Remove infected foliage and use labeled fungicides when needed.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.prevention: Mulch, rotate crops, stake plants, and avoid overhead irrigation.

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.symptoms: Fine stippling, yellowing leaves, webbing, and leaf bronzing under heavy infestation.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.treatment: Use miticides or insecticidal soaps according to label instructions and remove heavily infested leaves.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.prevention: Monitor leaf undersides, reduce plant stress, and conserve beneficial predators.

Tomato___Target_Spot.symptoms: Brown circular lesions with target-like rings on leaves and fruit.
Tomato___Target_Spot.treatment: Remove infected leaves and use fungicide if disease is spreading.
Tomato___Target_Spot.prevention: Improve airflow, avoid overhead irrigation, and rotate crops.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.symptoms: Upward leaf curling, yellowing, stunted growth, and reduced fruit set.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.treatment: No curative treatment exists; remove infected plants and manage whitefly vectors.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.prevention: Use resistant varieties, insect netting, reflective mulch, and whitefly control.

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.symptoms: Mottled light and dark green leaves, leaf distortion, and reduced plant vigor.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.treatment: Remove infected plants and disinfect tools and hands after handling.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.prevention: Use virus-free seed, resistant cultivars, and strict sanitation.

Tomato___healthy.prevention: Continue proper watering, staking, crop rotation, and routine scouting.
